seoTitle: Run and Export Payroll Reports in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Pull payroll summaries and tax liability reports in QuickBooks Online, filter by pay period, and export them to Excel or PDF.
intro: Reviewing monthly labor costs or pulling data for a tax filing window means finding the right report inside QuickBooks Online's payroll module rather than reconciling numbers by hand. This guide covers locating, filtering, and exporting your payroll reports.

steps.heading: Open the Reports hub
steps.body: Click Reports in the left navigation sidebar to reach the main reports directory.

steps.heading: Find the payroll section
steps.body: Scroll down to the Payroll category, or use the search box to jump directly to a report by name.

steps.heading: Select your target report
steps.body: Click into a specific report, such as Payroll Summary by Employee or Payroll Tax Liability.

steps.heading: Set your date range and filters
steps.body: Adjust the Date Range dropdown to your target pay period, then use the Filter funnel to narrow results by department, team, or individual worker.

steps.heading: Export and verify
steps.body: Click the export icon to choose Export to Excel or Export to PDF, then match total net pay figures against your bank feed outflows to confirm accuracy.

faq.question: Can I filter a payroll report by department instead of viewing the whole company?
faq.answer: Yes, the Filter funnel menu lets you narrow the display rows by specific departments, teams, or individual workers.

faq.question: What formats can I export payroll reports to?
faq.answer: You can export to either Excel or PDF directly from the export icon above the report table.

faq.question: How do I verify a payroll report is accurate?
faq.answer: Match the total net pay figures from the exported sheet against your bank feed outflows to confirm they balance.

faq.question: Do I need to manually recalculate the report after changing filters?
faq.answer: Clicking Run report after adjusting filters lets the engine instantly rebuild the ledger rows with your new parameters.
